cards.grammarNote: Korean adjectives conjugate like verbs. 맛있다 (to be delicious) → 맛있어요. No separate 'to be' is needed — the adjective verb carries the tense and politeness level.
맛있다 = 맛 (taste) + 있다 (to have/exist). 맛있어요 = it is delicious. 맛없어요 = it's not tasty / it's bland.
맛없다 = 맛 + 없다. 맛없어요 = no taste / not good. Used honestly but can be blunt — 별로예요 (it's so-so) is softer.
맵다 (to be spicy) → 매워요. ㅂ-irregular: ㅂ → 우 before vowel endings. 안 매워요 = it's not spicy.
달다 (to be sweet) → 달아요. ㄹ verb: 달 + 아요 = 달아요. 짜요 = salty (짜다 → 짜요).
짜다 → 짜요. 짜 has vowel ㅏ → -아요, contracting to 짜요. 싱거워요 = bland/not salty enough (싱겁다, ㅂ-irregular).
시다 (to be sour) → 셔요 (contracted) or 시어요. Common for vinegary or citrus foods. 쓴맛 = bitter taste.
뜨겁다 → 뜨거워요. ㅂ-irregular. 차가워요 = it's cold (차갑다 → 차가워요). 따뜻해요 = it's warm.
차갑다 → 차가워요. ㅂ-irregular. 차갑다 is for cold temperature; 시원해요 (refreshingly cool) is positive.
신선하다 (to be fresh) → 신선해요. 하다 adjective verb → 해요. Common for produce and seafood.
느끼하다 (to be greasy/fatty) → 느끼해요. 하다 adjective → 해요.
양 = portion/quantity. 이 = subject marker. 많다 (to be many/much) → 많아요. 적어요 = it's small/little (적다 → 적어요).
